9.reason: Incorrect: &str should be str, causing undefined behavior; may lead to compilation warning/error.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Error: scanf expects a char*, but &str gives a pointer to the array (type char(*)[100001]). Use str instead of &str.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Incorrect: &str should be str, causing undefined behavior; may lead to compilation warning/error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Incorrect argument type for scanf; &str is char (*)[100001], but %s expects char *, which causes a type mismatch compilation error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Incorrect argument type for %s format specifier; &str is of type char (*)[100001], should be str which decays to char *
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using &str is incorrect; str is already a pointer to char array. Should be scanf("%s", str);]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Using &str is incorrect; str is already a pointer to char array, should be scanf("%s", str);]

16.reason: Error: when i == len-1, str[i+1] accesses out-of-bounds memory. Should check i+1 < len.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Error: when i == len-1, str[i+1] accesses out-of-bounds memory. Should check i+1 < len.

26.reason: Error: same out-of-bounds access when i == len-1. Also this branch does not go to label e, causing the later "e-" to print incorrectly.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Error: same out-of-bounds access when i == len-1. Also this branch does not go to label e, causing the later "e-" to print incorrectly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Logical error: variable a is not updated here, leading to incorrect exponent calculation (a remains 0, prints e--1)

32.reason: Error: This line executes even if the else-if branch was taken, causing an extra "e-" after the digit printed there.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Error: This line executes even if the else-if branch was taken, causing an extra "e-" after the digit printed there.

33.reason: [If all digits after decimal are zero (not possible per problem but logic flawed), 'a' remains 0 leading to wrong exponent; also missing newline]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Error: For the else-if branch, a remains 0, so a-1 = -1 is printed, which is incorrect. Also for the case where no non-zero digit is found, a is 0, output is wrong.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [If all digits after decimal are zero (not possible per problem but logic flawed), 'a' remains 0 leading to wrong exponent; also missing newline]

28.reason: Missing assignment: a = i; should be set here to correct exponent.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing assignment: a = i; should be set here to correct exponent.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Missing a = i; and goto e; causes incorrect exponent calculation and improper control flow when the first non-zero digit is the last character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Missing decimal point and exponent handling when the non-zero digit is the last character; this branch prints only the digit without 'e' notation]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Missing decimal point and exponent handling when last non-zero digit is at end; also control falls through without setting 'a' properly]

37.reason: Unconditionally prints a decimal point, violating the output format requirement that integer bases must not contain a decimal point.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Unconditionally prints a decimal point, violating the output format requirement that integer bases must not contain a decimal point.

50.reason: [If input has no decimal point (though problem says it does), 'a' remains 0, leading to wrong exponent. Also, if number is like "5" (but per problem there's always a decimal), logic may fail. However, main issue: when non-zero integer part exists, trailing zeros after decimal may be printed, violating "no trailing zeros" rule. But more critically, if input is like "10.0", but problem states all digits are significant and no trailing zeros, so maybe not. However, the bigger flaw is in the zero-leading case above.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  If the input lacks a decimal point, a remains 0, resulting in an incorrect exponent of -1 instead of 0.
